mission.title: n8n: Error with the public API of n8n when registering a webhook
mission.description: Error with the public API of n8n when registering a webhook. This issue affects 5+ users.

steps.title: Identify n8n troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your n8n deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n -n n8n --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review n8n configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant n8n configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
kubectl get configmap -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=n8n
```
### Bug Description

I'm generating a workflow using the public API of n8n. Within it, I include a webhook. Once the webhook is activated, I get the path to run the automation, but the webhook does not respond.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Error with the public API of n8n when registering a webhook
steps.description:
Closing — this is the older parent ticket for the same webhook-registration failure tracked in #21614, which was fixed internally on 2026-03-23 (released ~2.14 onwards). Current shipping versions should not exhibit this. Please open a fresh issue if it reproduces on a current version.
```yaml
{
    "id": "webhook-1",
    "name": "Webhook",
    "type": "n8n-nodes-base.webhook",
    "typeVersion": 1,
    "position": [250, 300],
    "webhookId": "56d7c763-8c1c-4037-bd3a-77abe8e930e2",
    "parameters": {
        "path": "test-webhook",
        "httpMethod": "POST",
        "responseMode": "responseNode",
    }
}
```

steps.title: Upgrade n8n to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade n8n:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade n8n n8n/n8n --namespace n8n
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n n8n
helm list -n n8n
```

steps.title: Confirm Error with the public API of n8n when registering… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n -n n8n --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n n8n --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: Closing — this is the older parent ticket for the same webhook-registration failure tracked in #21614, which was fixed internally on 2026-03-23 (released ~2.14 onwards). Current shipping versions should not exhibit this. Please open a fresh issue if it reproduces on a current version.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with n8n installed or the issue environment reproducible.
